text: But ye shall offer an offering made by fire unto the LORD seven days: in the seventh day is an holy convocation: ye shall do no servile work therein.

label: law-wisdom
text: The proverbs of Solomon the son of David, king of Israel;

label: law-wisdom
text: To know wisdom and instruction; to perceive the words of understanding;

label: law-wisdom
text: To receive the instruction of wisdom, justice, and judgment, and equity;

label: law-wisdom
text: To give subtilty to the simple, to the young man knowledge and discretion.

label: law-wisdom
text: A wise man will hear, and will increase learning; and a man of understanding shall attain unto wise counsels:

label: law-wisdom
text: To understand a proverb, and the interpretation; the words of the wise, and their dark sayings.

label: law-wisdom
text: The fear of the LORD is the beginning of knowledge: but fools despise wisdom and instruction.

label: law-wisdom
text: My son, forget not my law; but let thine heart keep my commandments:

label: law-wisdom
text: For length of days, and long life, and peace, shall they add to thee.

label: law-wisdom
text: Let not mercy and truth forsake thee: bind them about thy neck; write them upon the table of thine heart:

label: law-wisdom
text: So shalt thou find favour and good understanding in the sight of God and man.

label: law-wisdom
text: Trust in the LORD with all thine heart; and lean not unto thine own understanding.

label: law-wisdom
text: In all thy ways acknowledge him, and he shall direct thy paths.

label: law-wisdom
text: Be not wise in thine own eyes: fear the LORD, and depart from evil.

label: law-wisdom
text: It shall be health to thy navel, and marrow to thy bones.

label: law-wisdom
text: Honour the LORD with thy substance, and with the firstfruits of all thine increase:

label: law-wisdom
text: So shall thy barns be filled with plenty, and thy presses shall burst out with new wine.

label: law-wisdom
text: My son, despise not the chastening of the LORD; neither be weary of his correction:

label: law-wisdom
text: For whom the LORD loveth he correcteth; even as a father the son in whom he delighteth.

label: law-wisdom
text: Go to the ant, thou sluggard; consider her ways, and be wise:

label: law-wisdom
text: Which having no guide, overseer, or ruler,

label: law-wisdom
text: Provideth her meat in the summer, and gathereth her food in the harvest.

label: law-wisdom
text: How long wilt thou sleep, O sluggard? when wilt thou arise out of thy sleep?

label: law-wisdom
text: Yet a little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to sleep:

label: law-wisdom
text: So shall thy poverty come as one that travelleth, and thy want as an armed man.

label: law-wisdom
text: Who can find a virtuous woman? for her price is far above rubies.

label: law-wisdom
text: The heart of her husband doth safely trust in her, so that he shall have no need of spoil.

label: law-wisdom
text: She will do him good and not evil all the days of her life.

label: law-wisdom
text: She seeketh wool, and flax, and worketh willingly with her hands.

label: law-wisdom
text: She is like the merchants’ ships; she bringeth her food from afar.

label: law-wisdom
text: She riseth also while it is yet night, and giveth meat to her household, and a portion to her maidens.

label: law-wisdom
text: She considereth a field, and buyeth it: with the fruit of her hands she planteth a vineyard.

label: law-wisdom
text: She girdeth her loins with strength, and strengtheneth her arms.

label: law-wisdom
text: She perceiveth that her merchandise is good: her candle goeth not out by night.

label: law-wisdom
text: She layeth her hands to the spindle, and her hands hold the distaff.

label: law-wisdom
text: She stretcheth out her hand to the poor; yea, she reacheth forth her hands to the needy.

label: law-wisdom
text: She is not afraid of the snow for her household: for all her household are clothed with scarlet.

label: law-wisdom
text: She maketh herself coverings of tapestry; her clothing is silk and purple.

label: law-wisdom
text: Her husband is known in the gates, when he sitteth among the elders of the land.

label: law-wisdom
text: She maketh fine linen, and selleth it; and delivereth girdles unto the merchant.

label: law-wisdom
text: Strength and honour are her clothing; and she shall rejoice in time to come.

label: law-wisdom
text: She openeth her mouth with wisdom; and in her tongue is the law of kindness.

label: law-wisdom
text: She looketh well to the ways of her household, and eateth not the bread of idleness.

label: law-wisdom
text: Her children arise up, and call her blessed; her husband also, and he praiseth her.

label: law-wisdom
text: Many daughters have done virtuously, but thou excellest them all.

label: law-wisdom
text: Favour is deceitful, and beauty is vain: but a woman that feareth the LORD, she shall be praised.

label: law-wisdom
text: Give her of the fruit of her hands; and let her own works praise her in the gates.

label: law-wisdom
note: the Shema: 'Hear, O Israel' -- preached law, vocative + second-person dense
text: Hear, O Israel: The LORD our God is one LORD:

label: law-wisdom
note: the Shema: 'Hear, O Israel' -- preached law, vocative + second-person dense
text: And thou shalt love the LORD thy God with all thine heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy might.

label: law-wisdom
note: the Shema: 'Hear, O Israel' -- preached law, vocative + second-person dense
text: And these words, which I command thee this day, shall be in thine heart:

label: law-wisdom
note: the Shema: 'Hear, O Israel' -- preached law, vocative + second-person dense
text: And thou shalt teach them diligently unto thy children, and shalt talk of them when thou sittest in thine house, and when thou walkest by the way, and when thou liest down, and when thou risest up.

label: law-wisdom
note: the Shema: 'Hear, O Israel' -- preached law, vocative + second-person dense
text: And thou shalt bind them for a sign upon thine hand, and they shall be as frontlets between thine eyes.

label: law-wisdom
note: the Shema: 'Hear, O Israel' -- preached law, vocative + second-person dense
text: And thou shalt write them upon the posts of thy house, and on thy gates.

label: law-wisdom
text: When thou dost lend thy brother any thing, thou shalt not go into his house to fetch his pledge.

label: law-wisdom
text: Thou shalt stand abroad, and the man to whom thou dost lend shall bring out the pledge abroad unto thee.

label: law-wisdom
text: And if the man be poor, thou shalt not sleep with his pledge:

label: law-wisdom
text: In any case thou shalt deliver him the pledge again when the sun goeth down, that he may sleep in his own raiment, and bless thee: and it shall be righteousness unto thee before the LORD thy God.

label: law-wisdom
text: Thou shalt not oppress an hired servant that is poor and needy, whether he be of thy brethren, or of thy strangers that are in thy land within thy gates:

label: law-wisdom
text: At his day thou shalt give him his hire, neither shall the sun go down upon it; for he is poor, and setteth his heart upon it: lest he cry against thee unto the LORD, and it be sin unto thee.

label: law-wisdom
text: And the LORD spake unto Moses in mount Sinai, saying,

label: law-wisdom
text: Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, When ye come into the land which I give you, then shall the land keep a sabbath unto the LORD.

label: law-wisdom
text: Six years thou shalt sow thy field, and six years thou shalt prune thy vineyard, and gather in the fruit thereof;

label: law-wisdom
text: But in the seventh year shall be a sabbath of rest unto the land, a sabbath for the LORD: thou shalt neither sow thy field, nor prune thy vineyard.

label: law-wisdom
text: That which groweth of its own accord of thy harvest thou shalt not reap, neither gather the grapes of thy vine undressed: for it is a year of rest unto the land.

label: law-wisdom
text: And the sabbath of the land shall be meat for you; for thee, and for thy servant, and for thy maid, and for thy hired servant, and for thy stranger that sojourneth with thee,

label: law-wisdom
text: And for thy cattle, and for the beast that are in thy land, shall all the increase thereof be meat.

label: law-wisdom
text: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

label: law-wisdom
text: Speak unto the children of Israel, and bid them that they make them fringes in the borders of their garments throughout their generations, and that they put upon the fringe of the borders a ribband of blue:

label: law-wisdom
text: And it shall be unto you for a fringe, that ye may look upon it, and remember all the commandments of the LORD, and do them; and that ye seek not after your own heart and your own eyes, after which ye use to go a whoring:

label: law-wisdom
text: That ye may remember, and do all my commandments, and be holy unto your God.

label: law-wisdom
text: I am the LORD your God, which brought you out of the land of Egypt, to be your God: I am the LORD your God.

label: law-wisdom
text: The proverbs of Solomon. A wise son maketh a glad father: but a foolish son is the heaviness of his mother.

label: law-wisdom
text: Treasures of wickedness profit nothing: but righteousness delivereth from death.

label: law-wisdom
text: The LORD will not suffer the soul of the righteous to famish: but he casteth away the substance of the wicked.

label: law-wisdom
text: He becometh poor that dealeth with a slack hand: but the hand of the diligent maketh rich.

label: law-wisdom
text: He that gathereth in summer is a wise son: but he that sleepeth in harvest is a son that causeth shame.

label: law-wisdom
text: Blessings are upon the head of the just: but violence covereth the mouth of the wicked.

label: law-wisdom
text: The memory of the just is blessed: but the name of the wicked shall rot.

label: law-wisdom
text: The wise in heart will receive commandments: but a prating fool shall fall.

label: law-wisdom
text: He that walketh uprightly walketh surely: but he that perverteth his ways shall be known.

label: law-wisdom
text: He that winketh with the eye causeth sorrow: but a prating fool shall fall.

label: law-wisdom
text: The mouth of a righteous man is a well of life: but violence covereth the mouth of the wicked.

label: law-wisdom
text: Hatred stirreth up strifes: but love covereth all sins.

label: law-wisdom
text: A soft answer turneth away wrath: but grievous words stir up anger.

label: law-wisdom
text: The tongue of the wise useth knowledge aright: but the mouth of fools poureth out foolishness.

label: law-wisdom
text: The eyes of the LORD are in every place, beholding the evil and the good.

label: law-wisdom
text: A wholesome tongue is a tree of life: but perverseness therein is a breach in the spirit.

label: law-wisdom
text: A fool despiseth his father’s instruction: but he that regardeth reproof is prudent.

label: law-wisdom
text: In the house of the righteous is much treasure: but in the revenues of the wicked is trouble.

label: law-wisdom
text: The lips of the wise disperse knowledge: but the heart of the foolish doeth not so.

label: law-wisdom
note: 'to every thing there is a season' -- wisdom poem
text: To every thing there is a season, and a time to every purpose under the heaven:

label: law-wisdom
note: 'to every thing there is a season' -- wisdom poem
text: A time to be born, and a time to die; a time to plant, and a time to pluck up that which is planted;

label: law-wisdom
note: 'to every thing there is a season' -- wisdom poem
text: A time to kill, and a time to heal; a time to break down, and a time to build up;

label: law-wisdom
note: 'to every thing there is a season' -- wisdom poem
text: A time to weep, and a time to laugh; a time to mourn, and a time to dance;

label: law-wisdom
note: 'to every thing there is a season' -- wisdom poem
text: A time to cast away stones, and a time to gather stones together; a time to embrace, and a time to refrain from embracing;

label: law-wisdom
note: 'to every thing there is a season' -- wisdom poem
text: A time to get, and a time to lose; a time to keep, and a time to cast away;

label: law-wisdom
note: 'to every thing there is a season' -- wisdom poem
text: A time to rend, and a time to sew; a time to keep silence, and a time to speak;

label: law-wisdom
note: 'to every thing there is a season' -- wisdom poem
text: A time to love, and a time to hate; a time of war, and a time of peace.

label: law-wisdom
text: Remember now thy Creator in the days of thy youth, while the evil days come not, nor the years draw nigh, when thou shalt say, I have no pleasure in them;

label: law-wisdom
text: While the sun, or the light, or the moon, or the stars, be not darkened, nor the clouds return after the rain:

label: law-wisdom
text: In the day when the keepers of the house shall tremble, and the strong men shall bow themselves, and the grinders cease because they are few, and those that look out of the windows be darkened,

label: law-wisdom
text: And the doors shall be shut in the streets, when the sound of the grinding is low, and he shall rise up at the voice of the bird, and all the daughters of musick shall be brought low;

label: law-wisdom
text: Also when they shall be afraid of that which is high, and fears shall be in the way, and the almond tree shall flourish, and the grasshopper shall be a burden, and desire shall fail: because man goeth to his long home, and the mourners go about the streets:

label: law-wisdom
text: Or ever the silver cord be loosed, or the golden bowl be broken, or the pitcher be broken at the fountain, or the wheel broken at the cistern.

label: law-wisdom
text: Then shall the dust return to the earth as it was: and the spirit shall return unto God who gave it.
